hero.heading: Your AI forgets everything between sessions
hero.subtitle: Context Vault gives it persistent memory that gets smarter over time. Local-first, works with every tool you already use.

leftPanelLines.text: Ready. Picking up where you left off.

stats.label: Setup time
stats.description: One command installs the server and connects it to your AI editor automatically.

stats.label: MCP client
stats.description: Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Zed, ChatGPT, VS Code. One vault, every tool.

stats.label: Private / Team / Public
stats.description: Local free forever. Pro for cloud sync. Team for shared vaults and SSO.

features.title: Persistent.
features.description: Decisions, patterns, and conventions live in your vault forever, not just for today's session.

features.title: Automatic.
features.description: Your AI retrieves the right context at session start. No prompting, no searching, no re-explaining.

features.title: Universal.
features.description: One vault. Every editor. Save in Claude Code, retrieve in Cursor. Context follows you, not the tool.

features.title: Local-first.
features.description: Plain markdown on your machine by default. Upgrade to Pro for cloud sync when you need it.

features.title: Recall tracking.
features.description: Your vault learns what's useful. See which entries get retrieved, which stay dormant, and what matters most.

features.title: Team vaults.
features.description: Share knowledge across your team. Onboarding context, architectural decisions, and project conventions in one place.

features.title: Vault-brain.
features.description: See your knowledge as a 3D neural network. Explore connections between entries and spot gaps in your context.

features.title: Agent rules.
features.description: Tell your agents what to save once. They learn your conventions and capture knowledge automatically after setup.

items.question: What is Context Vault?
items.answer: Context Vault is a local-first persistent memory layer for AI coding tools. It saves your decisions, patterns, and project context as plain markdown files, then automatically retrieves them in every new AI session. Free tier runs entirely on your machine. Pro adds cloud sync and recall tracking. Team adds shared vaults.

items.question: How long does setup take?
items.answer: About 2 minutes. Run 'npx context-vault setup' to install and configure. It auto-detects your AI editor and sets up the MCP connection. No account, no API key, no configuration files to edit.

items.question: Which AI tools are supported?
items.answer: Any MCP-compatible client works out of the box: Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Zed, and more. Browser-based tools like ChatGPT can connect via the REST API.

items.question: Where is my data stored?
items.answer: Free tier: on your machine in a folder you choose. Everything is plain markdown with a local SQLite index. Nothing is sent to the cloud. Pro tier: your vault syncs to Cloudflare R2 storage for multi-device access. You can export everything at any time.

items.question: Can I move or back up my vault?
items.answer: Yes. The local vault is just a folder of markdown files. Copy it, git push it, sync it with Dropbox, or move it to another machine. No export step needed.

items.question: Is the free tier really free forever?
items.answer: Yes. The local vault is MIT-licensed, runs on your machine, and has no limits on entries. Free tier will always exist. Pro ($9/mo) and Team ($29/mo + $9/seat) are optional upgrades for hosted features.

items.question: What do Pro and Team add?
items.answer: Pro adds hosted personal vault, multi-device sync via R2, recall tracking dashboard, and vault-brain 3D visualization. Team adds a shared vault for your team, member management, team-level agent rules, and SSO — included at no extra cost.
